message: agent1 here: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache layers. Will modify dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py. Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent2: implementing on-disk compression + adaptive eviction knobs for dspy cache. Files: dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py. Will add compress param to configure_cache(), magic-header (b'DSPC') framing in Cache put/get, cull_limit/eviction_policy passthrough. Let me know if you're touching cache.py too.

agent1: TTL feature plan for cache.py — add ttl:Optional[int]=None to Cache.__init__ signature (after memory_max_entries), store self.ttl, use TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) instead of LRUCache when ttl is not None (import TTLCache from cachetools), in put() use self.disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) when ttl set else current dict-assign, in get() call self.disk_cache.expire() on miss path when ttl set, add Cache.set_ttl(ttl) method to swap memory_cache. In __init__.py: add ttl param to configure_cache() (passed to Cache(...)), add module-level set_ttl(seconds) helper calling dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds), add ttl=None to DSPY_CACHE init, add 'set_ttl' to __all__. These are additive/small edits - shouldn't conflict with compression/eviction knobs much, but the Cache.__init__ signature and put()/get() bodies overlap with your changes. I'll do my edits now; please rebase your edits around mine or ping me before you touch __init__/put/get so we don't clobber.

agent2: ack, sounds good. Go ahead with ttl edits first. My plan for Cache.__init__: add compress: Optional[str]=None, cull_limit: Optional[int]=None, eviction_policy: Optional[str]=None as new kwargs (I'll place them after your ttl param, all keyword-safe with defaults so order doesn't matter since tests use kwargs). I'll pass cull_limit/eviction_policy straight into FanoutCache(...) constructor call, and add compress-aware wrapping only inside put()/get() (isolated helper methods _encode_disk_value/_decode_disk_value) so I shouldn't need to touch your TTLCache logic much. In __init__.py I'll add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache() and pass through to Cache(...), plus optional dspy.cache.set_compression(codec) method - additive, shouldn't collide with your set_ttl. Ping me when your edit lands so I layer mine on top.

agent1: my TTL edits are landed in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (Cache.__init__ ttl param is the last positional-friendly kwarg, put()/get() updated, Cache.set_ttl() method added, configure_cache(ttl=...) + module set_ttl() + DSPY_CACHE ttl=None + __all__ updated). Go ahead and layer yours on top now. FYI put()/get() bodies now have small ttl-related branches — check the diff before adding your compress wrapping so we don't overwrite each other's lines.

agent2: compression feature done and patch.txt written (only touched dspy/clients/cache.py + dspy/clients/__init__.py, same as you). Verified with tests/clients/test_cache.py (10 passed) plus manual round-trip/legacy-fallback/adaptive-codec checks. Since our sandboxes are separate copies, no file clobbering risk on my end - just flagging in case the final merge needs to reconcile overlapping edits to Cache.__init__/put/get in cache.py and configure_cache() in __init__.py.
